AISuggestion.name: AddBarometerSensor
AISuggestion.description: The specification requires a barometer sensor to provide barometric pressure, but the model does not include any Barometer block, part instance, or connection to deliver BarometerData to the Controller, even though the Controller declares this input.
AISuggestion.howToSolve: Add a Barometer sensor block with an output item typed by __type_BarometerData, instantiate it in the system, and connect its BarometerData output to Controller.BarometerData. If sensor behavior is modeled, add its acquisition state machine as well.

TURTLEGMODELING.SPECIFICATION: An oceanographic buoy is a meteorological monitoring system designed to collect and transmit data on sea state, wind conditions, and atmospheric parameters for weather monitoring and forecasting purposes. The buoy consists of sensors that measure environmental parameters, a controller that aggregates sensor data and computes derived parameters, and communication systems that enable transmission of both measured and computed data. The system to be designed is the software implementing the control logic of the buoy controller. The buoy shall transmit the following measurements via a VHF transmitter: buoy position, barometric pressure, wind direction, wind speed, wind gust, air temperature, seawater temperature, water level, relative humidity, direction of wave propagation, and wave energy spectrum. It shall be equipped with the following sensors: an IMU, a GNSS receiver, a barometer, an anemometer, an air temperature sensor, a water temperature sensor, and a humidity sensor. Wind gust shall be derived from anemometer data. Wave propagation direction and wave energy spectrum shall be derived from IMU data, and water level shall be derived from IMU and GNSS data. The model shall capture the control logic governing data acquisition, processing, and transmission, but shall not include the arithmetic computation of those derived values.

Remove unsupported satellite transmitter/Structure: The model contains a SatelliteTransmitter element, but the specification only defines transmission through a VHF transmitter. This extra transmitter is not supported by the system specification and overspecifies the architecture.
How to solve? Remove the SatelliteTransmitter block and its part instance, and keep the communication chain centered on the Controller and VHFTransmitter only.
Concerned views: BlockDiagram
component: SatelliteTransmitter new components : mutations: remove block SatelliteTransmitter

RemoveSatelliteTransmitter/Structure: The model contains a SatelliteTransmitter element, while the specification states that the buoy shall transmit measurements via a VHF transmitter only. This communication block is therefore not supported by the specification.
How to solve? Remove the SatelliteTransmitter block and its usage from the model, and keep VHFTransmitter as the only transmission component unless a satellite communication requirement is explicitly added to the specification.
Concerned views: BlockDiagram,SatelliteTransmitter component: SatelliteTransmitter new components :

infoparam.name: UML Note
infoparam.value:
*Executable* suggestion
- 348 seconds
- Semantic oracle evaluation = 9 (on SysML v2)
- Our evaluation: - Final model quality = 5 (remaining syntax errors) - Mutation quality = 8.8 (2 incorrect mutations out of 17)
Align transmitted data with specification/Structure: The model transmits raw IMU and GNSS values through the Controller to the VHFTransmitter, whereas the specification requires transmission of buoy position and does not require raw IMU/GNSS measurements to be part of the transmitted data. How to solve? Modify the Controller and VHFTransmitter interfaces and behavior so that the transmitted payload contains buoyPosition instead of raw IMU_xAcc, IMU_yAcc, IMU_zAcc, IMU_pitchRate, IMU_rollRate, IMU_yawRate, GNSS_x, GNSS_y, and GNSS_z. Keep IMU and GNSS data only as internal inputs used to acquire data and derive required values.
Concerned views: BlockDiagram,Controller,VHFTransmitter
component: Controller new components : buoyPosition mutations: add attribute int buoyPosition in Controller add attribute int buoyPosition in VHFTransmitter remove attribute IMU_xAcc in VHFTransmitter remove attribute IMU_yAcc in VHFTransmitter remove attribute IMU_zAcc in VHFTransmitter remove attribute IMU_pitchRate in VHFTransmitter remove attribute IMU_rollRate in VHFTransmitter remove attribute IMU_yawRate in VHFTransmitter remove attribute GNSS_x in VHFTransmitter remove attribute GNSS_y in VHFTransmitter remove attribute GNSS_z in VHFTransmitter remove signal toVHF in Controller add output signal toVHF(int buoyPosition, int airTemp, int waterTemp, int pressure, int windSpeed, int windDirection, int windGust, int humidity, int wavePropagationDirection, int waveEnergySpectrum, int waterLevel) in Controller remove signal fromController in VHFTransmitter add input signal fromController(int buoyPosition, int airTemp, int waterTemp, int pressure, int windSpeed, int windDirection, int windGust, int humidity, int wavePropagationDirection, int waveEnergySpectrum, int waterLevel) in VHFTransmitter remove transition in Controller from sendingMeasurements to readingSensorData add transition in Controller from sendingMeasurements to readingSensorData with "toVHF(buoyPosition, airTemp, waterTemp, pressure, windSpeed, windDirection, windGust, humidity, wavePropagationDirection, waveEnergySpectrum, waterLevel)"

Line.value: - Mutation quality = 8.8 (2 incorrect mutations out of 17)

The model transmits raw IMU and GNSS values through the Controller to the VHFTransmitter, whereas the specification requires transmission of buoy position and does not require raw IMU/GNSS measurements to be part of the transmitted data.

Modify the Controller and VHFTransmitter interfaces and behavior so that the transmitted payload contains buoyPosition instead of raw IMU_xAcc, IMU_yAcc, IMU_zAcc, IMU_pitchRate, IMU_rollRate, IMU_yawRate, GNSS_x, GNSS_y, and GNSS_z.
Keep IMU and GNSS data only as internal inputs used to acquire data and derive required values.

Well-formed executable suggestion
RemoveSatelliteTransmitter/Structure: The model contains a SatelliteTransmitter block and instance, but the specification only requires a VHF transmitter for data transmission. This introduces an extra communication element not defined in the specification.
How to solve? Remove the SatelliteTransmitter block and its part instance from the model, and keep VHFTransmitter as the sole transmission component.
Concerned views: BlockDiagram
component: SatelliteTransmitter new components : mutations: remove block SatelliteTransmitter

Line.value: RemoveSatelliteTransmitter/Structure: The model contains a SatelliteTransmitter block and instance, but the specification only requires a VHF transmitter for data transmission.
This introduces an extra communication element not defined in the specification.
How to solve? Remove the SatelliteTransmitter block and its part instance from the model, and keep VHFTransmitter as the sole transmission component.

infoparam.name: UML Note
infoparam.value:
executable suggestion
- 324 seconds
- Semantic oracle evaluation = 9 (on SysML v2) - Our evaluation: - Final model = 9 (syntactically incorrect but the patch is easy - align send/receive actions on the new signals signature) - Mutations = 8 (all of them are correct but two mutations, modifying actions on signals, are missing)
Align transmitted data with specified VHF outputs/Structure: The model transmits raw IMU variables and raw GNSS coordinates through the Controller to the VHFTransmitter, whereas the specification requires transmission of buoy position and only the listed measured or derived environmental outputs. This is a coherency issue between the specified communication content and the modeled transmitted payload.
How to solve? Modify the transmitted data exchanged between Controller and VHFTransmitter so it contains buoyPosition, barometric pressure, wind direction, wind speed, wind gust, air temperature, seawater temperature, water level, relative humidity, direction of wave propagation, and wave energy spectrum only. Remove raw IMU and raw GNSS fields from the VHF payload while keeping the controller logic that acquires sensor data and derives the required values without modeling their arithmetic computation.
Concerned views: BlockDiagram,Controller,VHFTransmitter
component: Controller new components : buoyPosition mutations: add attribute int buoyPosition in Controller add attribute int buoyPosition in VHFTransmitter remove connection from toVHF in Controller to fromController in VHFTransmitter remove signal toVHF in Controller add output signal toVHF(int buoyPosition, int pressure, int windDirection, int windSpeed, int windGust, int airTemp, int waterTemp, int waterLevel, int humidity, int wavePropagationDirection, int waveEnergySpectrum) in Controller remove signal fromController in VHFTransmitter add input signal fromController(int buoyPosition, int pressure, int windDirection, int windSpeed, int windGust, int airTemp, int waterTemp, int waterLevel, int humidity, int wavePropagationDirection, int waveEnergySpectrum) in VHFTransmitter add connection from toVHF in Controller to fromController in VHFTransmitter remove attribute IMU_xAcc in VHFTransmitter remove attribute IMU_yAcc in VHFTransmitter remove attribute IMU_zAcc in VHFTransmitter remove attribute IMU_pitchRate in VHFTransmitter remove attribute IMU_rollRate in VHFTransmitter remove attribute IMU_yawRate in VHFTransmitter remove attribute GNSS_x in VHFTransmitter remove attribute GNSS_y in VHFTransmitter remove attribute GNSS_z in VHFTransmitter

Line.value: - Final model = 9 (syntactically incorrect but the patch is easy - align send/receive actions on the new signals signature)
- Mutations = 8 (all of them are correct but two mutations, modifying actions on signals, are missing)
Align transmitted data with specified VHF outputs/Structure:

The model transmits raw IMU variables and raw GNSS coordinates through the Controller to the VHFTransmitter, whereas the specification requires transmission of buoy position and only the listed measured or derived environmental outputs.
This is a coherency issue between the specified communication content and the modeled transmitted payload.

Modify the transmitted data exchanged between Controller and VHFTransmitter so it contains buoyPosition, barometric pressure, wind direction, wind speed, wind gust, air temperature, seawater temperature, water level, relative humidity, direction of wave propagation, and wave energy spectrum only.
Remove raw IMU and raw GNSS fields from the VHF payload while keeping the controller logic that acquires sensor data and derives the required values without modeling their arithmetic computation.

add attribute int buoyPosition in VHFTransmitter

infoparam.name: UML Note
infoparam.value:
246 s
Semantic oracle evaluation = 8
Our evaluation = 10 (both model and mutations)
MissingBarometerSensor/Structure: The specification requires the buoy to be equipped with a barometer and to transmit barometric pressure, but the model does not contain any Barometer block, barometer part instance, or connection providing pressure data to the Controller. Although __type_BarometerData and Controller.BarometerData are declared, the required sensor itself is absent from the model.
How to solve? Add a dedicated Barometer block and an instantiated Barometer part that emits BarometerData, then connect it to Controller.BarometerData. Also add the corresponding acquisition behavior for this sensor so the controller can receive pressure measurements before transmission.
Concerned views: BlockDiagram Controller component: Controller new components: Barometer
mutations: add block Barometer add attribute int pressure in Barometer add output signal BarometerData(int pressure) in Barometer add state acquiringMeasurements in Barometer add transition in Barometer from start to acquiringMeasurements add action on signal sendBarometerData in Barometer with BarometerData(pressure) add transition in Barometer from acquiringMeasurements to sendBarometerData add transition in Barometer from sendBarometerData to acquiringMeasurements add private synchronous link between Barometer and Controller add connection from BarometerData in Barometer to BarometerData in Controller in private synchronous link

Line.value: MissingBarometerSensor/Structure: The specification requires the buoy to be equipped with a barometer and to transmit barometric pressure, but the model does not contain any Barometer block, barometer part instance, or connection providing pressure data to the Controller. Although __type_BarometerData and Controller.BarometerData are declared, the required sensor itself is absent from the model.

How to solve? Add a dedicated Barometer block and an instantiated Barometer part that emits BarometerData, then connect it to Controller.BarometerData. Also add the corresponding acquisition behavior for this sensor so the controller can receive pressure measurements before transmission.
add attribute int pressure in Barometer
add output signal BarometerData(int pressure) in Barometer
add transition in Barometer from start to acquiringMeasurements
add action on signal sendBarometerData in Barometer with BarometerData(pressure)
add transition in Barometer from acquiringMeasurements to sendBarometerData
add transition in Barometer from sendBarometerData to acquiringMeasurements
add private synchronous link between Barometer and Controller
add connection from BarometerData in Barometer to BarometerData in Controller in private synchronous link

infoparam.name: UML Note
infoparam.value:
*Well-formed executable* suggestion
- 229 seconds
- Semantic oracle evaluation = 10 (on the SysML v2)
- Our evaluation: - Final model = 8 (Barometer's SMD misses) - Mutation script = 10 (perfectly implements the textual description of the suggestion)
AddBarometerSensor/Structure: The specification requires a barometer sensor and transmission of barometric pressure, but the model does not include any barometer block, barometer part, or connection providing `BarometerData` to the controller. This is a coherency issue because `Controller` already expects `BarometerData`, yet no modeling element produces it.
How to solve? Add a barometer sensing block and corresponding part instance that outputs `BarometerData`, then connect it to `Controller.BarometerData`. This will make pressure acquisition explicit and consistent with the specified sensor set and transmitted measurements.
Concerned views: BlockDiagram,Controller component: Controller new components : Barometer
mutations: add block Barometer add attribute int pressure in Barometer add output signal BarometerData(int pressure) in Barometer add private synchronous link between Barometer and Controller add connection from BarometerData in Barometer to BarometerData in Controller in private synchronous link

Line.value: - Semantic oracle evaluation = 10 (on the SysML v2)
- Mutation script = 10 (perfectly implements the textual description of the suggestion)

This is a coherency issue because `Controller` already expects `BarometerData`, yet no modeling element produces it.
Add a barometer sensing block and corresponding part instance that outputs `BarometerData`, then connect it to `Controller.BarometerData`.
This will make pressure acquisition explicit and consistent with the specified sensor set and transmitted measurements.

add output signal BarometerData(int pressure) in Barometer
add private synchronous link between Barometer and Controller

infoparam.name: UML Note
infoparam.value:
*Well-formed executable* suggestion
- 284 seconds
- Semantic oracle evaluation = 10 (on the SysML v2)
- Our evaluation: - Final model = 8 (Barometer's SMD misses) - Mutation script = 10 (perfectly implements the textual description of the suggestion)
AddBarometerSensor/Structure: The specification requires a barometer sensor to provide barometric pressure, but the model has no Barometer block, no Barometer part instance, and no connection sending barometer data to the controller, even though Controller.BarometerData is declared.
How to solve? Add a Barometer block with an output item of type __type_BarometerData, instantiate it as a system part, and connect its BarometerData output to Controller.BarometerData. This completes the required sensing chain while keeping arithmetic computation details outside the model.
Concerned views: BlockDiagram,Controller component: Controller new components : Barometer
mutations: add block Barometer add attribute int pressure in Barometer add output signal BarometerData(int pressure) in Barometer add private synchronous link between Barometer and Controller add connection from BarometerData in Barometer to BarometerData in Controller in private synchronous link

- Mutation script = 10 (perfectly implements the textual description of the suggestion)

How to solve? Add a Barometer block with an output item of type __type_BarometerData, instantiate it as a system part, and connect its BarometerData output to Controller.BarometerData.
This completes the required sensing chain while keeping arithmetic computation details outside the model.

infoparam.name: UML Note
infoparam.value:
*Executable* suggestion
- 232 seconds
- Semantic oracle evaluation = 10 (on the SysML v2)
- Our evaluation: - Final model = 5 (Barometer's SMD, attributes and connection misses) - Mutation script = 8.3 (5 AMULET commands out of 6 are correct)
AddBarometerSensor/Structure: The specification requires a barometer sensor to provide barometric pressure, but the model does not contain any Barometer block, barometer part instance, or connection sending barometer data to the Controller. This is a coherency issue because the Controller already declares BarometerData as an input while the corresponding sensor is missing from the architecture.
How to solve? Add a Barometer block producing BarometerData, instantiate a Barometer part, and connect it to Controller.BarometerData so pressure acquisition matches the specification.
Concerned views: BlockDiagram,Controller
component: Controller new components : Barometer mutations: add block Barometer attach Barometer to Buoy add attribute int pressure in Barometer add output signal BarometerData(int pressure) in Barometer add private synchronous link between Barometer and Controller add connection from BarometerData in Barometer to BarometerData in Controller in private synchronous link

- Final model = 5 (Barometer's SMD, attributes and connection misses)
- Mutation script = 8.3 (5 AMULET commands out of 6 are correct)

This is a coherency issue because the Controller already declares BarometerData as an input while the corresponding sensor is missing from the architecture.
Add a Barometer block producing BarometerData, instantiate a Barometer part, and connect it to Controller.BarometerData so pressure acquisition matches the specification.

infoparam.name: UML Note
infoparam.value:
*Well-formed executable* suggestion
- 303 seconds
- Semantic oracle evaluation = 10 (on SysML v2)
- Our evaluation = 10, both final model and mutation script
AddBarometerSensor/Structure: The specification requires a barometer sensor to provide barometric pressure, but the model does not include any Barometer block, part instance, or connection to deliver BarometerData to the Controller, even though the Controller declares this input.
How to solve? Add a Barometer sensor block with an output item typed by __type_BarometerData, instantiate it in the system, and connect its BarometerData output to Controller.BarometerData. If sensor behavior is modeled, add its acquisition state machine as well.
Concerned views: BlockDiagram,Controller
component: Controller new components : Barometer mutations: add block Barometer add attribute int pressure in Barometer add output signal BarometerData(int pressure) in Barometer add state acquiringMeasurements in Barometer add transition in Barometer from start to acquiringMeasurements add action on signal sendBarometerData in Barometer with BarometerData(pressure) add transition in Barometer from acquiringMeasurements to sendBarometerData add transition in Barometer from sendBarometerData to acquiringMeasurements add private synchronous link Barometer_Controller between Barometer and Controller add connection from BarometerData in Barometer to BarometerData in Controller

Line.value: - Our evaluation = 10, both final model and mutation script

The specification requires a barometer sensor to provide barometric pressure, but the model does not include any Barometer block, part instance, or connection to deliver BarometerData to the Controller, even though the Controller declares this input.
Add a Barometer sensor block with an output item typed by __type_BarometerData, instantiate it in the system, and connect its BarometerData output to Controller.BarometerData.
If sensor behavior is modeled, add its acquisition state machine as well.
